title: Microdon dubius
taxonomy: Microdon dubius is classified within the family Syrphidae, which includes hoverflies. This family is part of the larger order Diptera, which comprises all true flies. Within the Diptera order, Syrphidae is one of the six superfamilies that make up the infraorder Schizophora. Microdon dubius is a species of hoverfly, specifically within the genus Microdon. The classification of Microdon dubius places it within the kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, and class Insecta.

intro: Microdon dubius is a plant species, commonly known as the "yellow false foxglove", that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. The health benefits of Microdon dubius are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of this plant include diterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
